subject: FW: For Jeffrey Epstein
body:
From: egneurocog@aol.com [mailto:egneurocog@aol.com] 
Sent: Friday, May 30, 2008 3:52 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Re: For Jeffrey Epstein

 

Dear Jeffrey,

Trust things are under control with you. Colly Myers and I had a very
productive visit earlier this week and have in fact jump-started the
project along the lines of our earlier discussions. As one of the UK's
top software architects from the real world (as opposed to academe),
Colly felt that the idea of grafting macroscopic brainlike architecture
onto micro- and midlevel neural-net circuitry is quite compelling and
has started designing precisely that as a result of our discussions. He
lives in London and plans to eventually bring some of the best software
talent in the UK into the project. So am delighted that we are finally
past the planning stage and are doing actual work. If you want to, we
will keep you posted, and perhaps you will meet Colly during his next
visit to NYC, whenever that may be. 

Best wishes,

Elkhonon

Elkhonon Goldberg, Ph.D., ABPP

Clinical Professor of Neurology
New York University School of Medicine

Diplomate
American Board of Professional Psychology
in Clinical Neuropsychology

315 West 57th Street
Suite 401
New York, NY 10019
USA

tel +1 212 541 6412
fax +1 212 246 8916
egneurocog@aol.com
elkhonon.goldberg@med.nyu.edu
www.elkhonongoldberg.com



-----Original Message-----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: egneurocog@aol.com
Sent: Wed, 14 May 2008 2:29 pm
Subject: RE: For Jeffrey Epstein

terrific

 

From: egneurocog@aol.com [mailto:egneurocog@aol.com
<mailto:egneurocog@aol.com?> ] 
Sent: Wednesday, May 14, 2008 2:29 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Re: For Jeffrey Epstein

 

Colly Myers confirmed and is looking forward

EG

Elkhonon Goldberg, Ph.D., ABPP

Clinical Professor of Neurology
New York University School of Medicine

Diplomate
American Board of Professional Psychology
in Clinical Neuropsychology

315 West 57th Street
Suite 401
New York, NY 10019
USA

tel +1 212 541 6412
fax +1 212 246 8916
egneurocog@aol.com
elkhonon.goldberg@med.nyu.edu
www.elkhonongoldberg.com <http://www.elkhonongoldberg.com/> 



-----Original Message-----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: egneurocog@aol.com
Sent: Wed, 14 May 2008 12:00 pm
Subject: RE: For Jeffrey Epstein

Yes!!

 

From: egneurocog@aol.com [mailto:egneurocog@aol.com
<mailto:egneurocog@aol.com?> ] 
Sent: Wednesday, May 14, 2008 11:44 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Re: For Jeffrey Epstein

 

OK, will do. Shall we assume that the meeting is on sometime on Mon May
26?

Thanks,

EG

Elkhonon Goldberg, Ph.D., ABPP

Clinical Professor of Neurology
New York University School of Medicine

Diplomate
American Board of Professional Psychology
in Clinical Neuropsychology

315 West 57th Street
Suite 401
New York, NY 10019
USA

tel +1 212 541 6412
fax +1 212 246 8916
egneurocog@aol.com
elkhonon.goldberg@med.nyu.edu
www.elkhonongoldberg.com <http://www.elkhonongoldberg.com/> 



-----Original Message-----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: egneurocog@aol.com
Cc: kellens@earthlink.net
Sent: Wed, 14 May 2008 11:36 am
Subject: RE: For Jeffrey Epstein

Jeffrey would like you to call Sarah next Sat. May 24th at (917 855
3363) to organize for Monday May 26th.

 

Thank you!

 

From: egneurocog@aol.com [mailto:egneurocog@aol.com
<mailto:egneurocog@aol.com?> ] 
Sent: Wednesday, May 14, 2008 10:36 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Re: For Jeffrey Epstein

 

Hi Lesley,

Colly Myers will be in NYC just for the weekend visiting his relatives
and could meet on Monday, May 26 anytime as long as he can be back at
The Four Seasons Hotel before 5pm to go to the airport and back to
London. He and I were supposed to meet in my office at 12pm and talk
about our project, but we can meet with Jeffrey instead - or before, or
after. I think his whole Monday before 5pm is flexible. Please advise.

EG

Elkhonon Goldberg, Ph.D., ABPP

Clinical Professor of Neurology
New York University School of Medicine

Diplomate
American Board of Professional Psychology
in Clinical Neuropsychology

315 West 57th Street
Suite 401
New York, NY 10019
USA

tel +1 212 541 6412
fax +1 212 246 8916
egneurocog@aol.com
elkhonon.goldberg@med.nyu.edu
www.elkhonongoldberg.com <http://www.elkhonongoldberg.com/> 



-----Original Message-----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: egneurocog@aol.com
Sent: Wed, 14 May 2008 9:50 am
Subject: RE: For Jeffrey Epstein

Dr. Goldberg!  Jeffrey said he would definitely like to meet Colly.
Please let us know of his schedule!

 

Thank you, Lesley

 

From: egneurocog@aol.com [mailto:egneurocog@aol.com
<mailto:egneurocog@aol.com?> ] 
Sent: Tuesday, May 13, 2008 5:30 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: For Jeffrey Epstein

 

Greetings Jeffrey,

Someone will be passing through town whom you may be interested in
meeting. Nicholas (Colly) Myers is the former (in fact, first) CEO of
Symbian, a British software company. Colly and his older brother Sir
David Potter are good examples of the "best and the brightest" leaving
university-based science to become high-tech entrepreneurs. Colly was
the chief software architect at Symbian, and distinct parallels exist
between the evolution of his interests and those of Jeff Hawkins. Colly
and I intersected some time ago, and we are discussing a collaboration
converging his talent in software design and my knowledge of the brain -
not dissimilar from our discussions. Colly will be in New York very
briefly in the end of May and I wonder if you would like to meet him if
your schedules mesh. Please let me know.

Best wishes,

Elkhonon

Elkhonon Goldberg, Ph.D., ABPP

Clinical Professor of Neurology
New York University School of Medicine

Diplomate
American Board of Professional Psychology
in Clinical Neuropsychology

315 West 57th Street
Suite 401
New York, NY 10019
USA

tel +1 212 541 6412
fax +1 212 246 8916
egneurocog@aol.com
elkhonon.goldberg@med.nyu.edu
www.elkhonongoldberg.com <http://www.elkhonongoldberg.com/> 



-----Original Message-----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: egneurocog@aol.com
Sent: Fri, 9 May 2008 4:30 pm
Subject: RE: Jeffrey Epstein

Let me speak with Jeffrey and I will get back to you!

 

From: egneurocog@aol.com [mailto:egneurocog@aol.com
<mailto:egneurocog@aol.com?> ] 
Sent: Friday, May 09, 2008 4:11 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Re: Jeffrey Epstein

 

Hi Lesley,

As you may know, Jeffrey and I have continued our discussions about
launching a brain modeling project. I would like to introduce to Jeffrey
Prof. Larry Abbott, Director of the Theoretical Neurobiology Program at
Columbia University and the author of the authoritative text on
computational neuroscience. Please let me know if we can set up a
meeting at Jeffrey's convenience.

Best wishes,

EG

Elkhonon Goldberg, Ph.D., ABPP

Clinical Professor of Neurology
New York University School of Medicine

Diplomate
American Board of Professional Psychology
in Clinical Neuropsychology

315 West 57th Street
Suite 401
New York, NY 10019
USA

tel +1 212 541 6412
fax +1 212 246 8916
egneurocog@aol.com
elkhonon.goldberg@med.nyu.edu
www.elkhonongoldberg.com <http://www.elkhonongoldberg.com/> 

-----Original Message-----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: egneurocog@aol.com
Sent: Tue, 6 May 2008 10:25 am
Subject: Jeffrey Epstein

Hello Dr. Goldberg.  Jeffrey would like to speak with you.  Could you
please give him a call at the office?  212 750 9895.

 

Thanks, Lesley

subject: FW: For Jeffrey Epstein
body:
From: egneurocog@aol.com [mailto:egneurocog@aol.com] 
Sent: Tuesday, May 13, 2008 5:30 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: For Jeffrey Epstein

 

Greetings Jeffrey,

Someone will be passing through town whom you may be interested in
meeting. Nicholas (Colly) Myers is the former (in fact, first) CEO of
Symbian, a British software company. Colly and his older brother Sir
David Potter are good examples of the "best and the brightest" leaving
university-based science to become high-tech entrepreneurs. Colly was
the chief software architect at Symbian, and distinct parallels exist
between the evolution of his interests and those of Jeff Hawkins. Colly
and I intersected some time ago, and we are discussing a collaboration
converging his talent in software design and my knowledge of the brain -
not dissimilar from our discussions. Colly will be in New York very
briefly in the end of May and I wonder if you would like to meet him if
your schedules mesh. Please let me know.

Best wishes,

Elkhonon

Elkhonon Goldberg, Ph.D., ABPP

Clinical Professor of Neurology
New York University School of Medicine

Diplomate
American Board of Professional Psychology
in Clinical Neuropsychology

315 West 57th Street
Suite 401
New York, NY 10019
USA

tel +1 212 541 6412
fax +1 212 246 8916
egneurocog@aol.com
elkhonon.goldberg@med.nyu.edu
www.elkhonongoldberg.com



-----Original Message-----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: egneurocog@aol.com
Sent: Fri, 9 May 2008 4:30 pm
Subject: RE: Jeffrey Epstein

Let me speak with Jeffrey and I will get back to you!

 

From: egneurocog@aol.com [mailto:egneurocog@aol.com
<mailto:egneurocog@aol.com?> ] 
Sent: Friday, May 09, 2008 4:11 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Re: Jeffrey Epstein

 

Hi Lesley,

As you may know, Jeffrey and I have continued our discussions about
launching a brain modeling project. I would like to introduce to Jeffrey
Prof. Larry Abbott, Director of the Theoretical Neurobiology Program at
Columbia University and the author of the authoritative text on
computational neuroscience. Please let me know if we can set up a
meeting at Jeffrey's convenience.

Best wishes,

EG

Elkhonon Goldberg, Ph.D., ABPP

Clinical Professor of Neurology
New York University School of Medicine

Diplomate
American Board of Professional Psychology
in Clinical Neuropsychology
